testing_strategy.overview: Comprehensive strategies for running, debugging, and fixing unit tests after package upgrades. This guide covers test execution, failure analysis, and systematic debugging approaches.

commands.python: # Remove old virtual environment rm -rf venv/ # Create fresh virtual environment python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
commands.nodejs: # Remove node_modules and lock files rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json # Fresh install npm install
commands.java: # Clean build artifacts mvn clean # Or for Gradle gradle clean

step.importance: Critical to install the exact versions from the PR to reproduce failures

strategy.name: full_suite_first
strategy.description: Run complete test suite to get overview
strategy.purpose: - Get total count of failures - Identify failure patterns - Understand scope of work

strategy.name: isolated_test_execution
strategy.description: Run individual failing tests for detailed analysis
strategy.purpose: - Focus on specific failure - Get detailed error output - Faster iteration during fixes

strategy.name: verbose_output
strategy.purpose: - See full stack traces - View print statements - Understand test flow

strategy.name: stop_on_first_failure
strategy.description: Stop after first failure for quick debugging
strategy.purpose: - Fix one issue at a time - Faster feedback loop - Avoid overwhelming output

method.name: file_redirect
method.description: Save test output to file for review

category.name: import_failures
category.analysis_approach: - Check if module was renamed or moved - Verify package is installed correctly - Look for import path changes in package docs - Check if submodule structure changed

category.name: api_signature_failures

symptoms.symptom: TypeError: takes X arguments but Y were given

category.analysis_approach: - Compare old vs new function signatures - Check for new required parameters - Look for removed or renamed parameters - Verify parameter order hasn't changed

example_errors.error: TypeError: function() missing 1 required positional argument: 'new_param'
TypeError: function() got an unexpected keyword argument 'old_param'
TypeError: function() takes 2 positional arguments but 3 were given

category.name: assertion_failures
category.analysis_approach: - Check if package behavior changed - Verify if return types changed - Look for changes in default values - Check if output format changed

example_errors.error: AssertionError: Expected dict but got object

category.name: attribute_errors
category.analysis_approach: - Check if attribute was renamed - Verify if attribute was removed - Look for moved attributes - Check if access pattern changed (property vs method)

category.name: deprecation_warnings
category.analysis_approach: - Note the warning message - Find recommended replacement - Plan to update before next major version - May not cause immediate failures but should be fixed

example_warnings.warning: FutureWarning: This feature will be removed in version 3.0

component.name: error_message
component.importance: Often contains specific details about what went wrong

component.name: call_stack
component.description: Sequence of function calls leading to the error
component.reading_strategy: - Start from the bottom (where error occurred) - Work upward to understand call chain - Identify first frame in your code (vs library code)

component.name: file_and_line
component.importance: Tells you exactly where to look

component.name: code_context
component.description: The actual line of code that failed
component.importance: Shows what operation was being attempted

step.action: Identify the error type and message
step.details: This tells you what category of problem you're dealing with

step.action: Find the first frame in your code
step.details: Skip library/framework frames and focus on your application code
Look at the exact code that caused the error
Follow the call stack to understand how you got there

step.action: Look for patterns across multiple failures
step.details: If multiple tests fail similarly, there's likely a common root cause

technique.name: binary_search_debugging
technique.description: Narrow down the problem by testing intermediate states

steps.step: If multiple changes, test each change individually
Identify which specific change causes the failure

technique.when_to_use: When multiple packages were upgraded or many changes were made

technique.name: minimal_reproduction
technique.description: Create smallest possible test case that reproduces the issue

steps.step: Copy failing test to isolated file

technique.benefits: - Easier to understand - Faster to test - Clearer what's causing the issue

technique.name: comparison_debugging
technique.description: Compare behavior between old and new versions

approach.step: Create test environment with old version
Run same code and capture output
Run same code and capture output

tools.tool: pdb/debugger to step through both versions
print statements to log intermediate values

technique.name: documentation_driven_debugging
technique.description: Use package documentation to understand expected behavior

steps.step: Find documentation for the failing function/class
Compare your usage with documented usage
Adjust your code to match documented API

technique.name: interactive_debugging
technique.description: Use debugger to step through code execution

python.usage: import pdb; pdb.set_trace() # Or use breakpoint() in Python 3.7+

isolation_strategy.principle: Fix one test at a time
isolation_strategy.rationale: - Easier to understand the specific issue - Faster feedback loop - Prevents confusion from multiple failures - Allows incremental progress

step.criteria: - Fewest dependencies - Clearest error message - Most isolated functionality

step.analysis: - Read the error message carefully - Examine the stack trace - Review the test code - Check what's being tested

step.approach: - Make minimal changes - Focus on the specific issue - Don't try to fix multiple things at once

step.verification: - Run the test again - Confirm it passes - Run related tests to check for regressions

step.details: Repeat the process for the next test

grouping_related_failures.description: Some failures have the same root cause and can be fixed together

approach.step: Verify all related tests now pass
This is more efficient than fixing each test individually

grouping_related_failures.example: If 10 tests fail with "ImportError: cannot import 'OldClass'", fixing the import statement once will fix all 10 tests.

continuous_verification.principle: Run full test suite after each fix
continuous_verification.rationale: Ensure your fix doesn't break other tests

workflow.step: Check that no new failures appeared
If new failures, investigate and fix

signs.sign: Tests that previously passed now fail

response.step: Understand why it caused the regression
Adjust the fix to avoid the regression
Consider if a different approach is needed

section.name: fixing
section.item: ✓ Tests fixed one at a time (or by group)
